column2: Sustained Return of Spontaneous Circulation (ROSC): defined as the documented presence of a measurable pulse and blood pressure upon emergency department arrival.
Survival to Awakening: This will be defined as time from arrest to the day a patient is able to obey verbal commands after emergency department arrival.
Survival to Withdrawal of Care: This will be defined as time from arrest to the day care is withdrawn after emergency department arrival.

In-Hospital Morbidity: The number of hospital days and time interval from 911call to patient death will be described for all hospitalized patients as measures of in-hospital morbidity after resuscitation.
